mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack increases recession probabilities, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, invalidating the risk-off signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and rising consumer prices indicate stagflationary pressure, forcing investors to devalue equities and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL MoM fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.04 over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals peak corporate earnings momentum, driving short-term equity price appreciation and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO MoM growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of late-cycle expansion periods, contradicting the earnings momentum channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls expand household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS MoM changes remains below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4 percent, negating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, boosting equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST growth rates yields a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 4-year forward testing period.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising unemployment alongside higher federal funds rates signals policy-induced economic slowdown, forcing equity deleveraging and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 55 percent of observations during tightening regimes when both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE rise simultaneously, invalidating the slowdown channel.
